text: Git branching is an important part of version control. It allows you to work on new features without affecting the main code. You can create a feature branch, make changes, and test them safely. Once your work is ready, you merge it back into the main branch. In many teams, this is done through a pull request. A pull request lets others review your code before it is added. This workflow helps prevent errors and keeps the project organized. By using branches, you can collaborate with others easily, experiment with new ideas, and maintain a stable version of your project at all times.
